title: uragoga reniformis
side_effects: Uragoga reniformis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. Prolonged use of the plant may also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as electrolyte imbalances. In some cases, uragoga reniformis may cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching, or interact with other medications to produce adverse effects. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant to avoid these potential side effects.
